expectations.text: Contains a concrete analogy using familiar real-world concepts
expectations.evidence: Restaurant/pizza ordering analogy, plus delivery app analogy. Two analogies used.

expectations.text: Explicitly states where the analogy breaks down
expectations.evidence: No mention of analogy limitations or where it diverges from how async/await actually works.

expectations.text: Includes a visual (tldraw, Mermaid, or structured)
expectations.evidence: No visual produced. Text and code blocks only.

expectations.text: The visual teaches something the text alone does not

expectations.evidence: Starts with analogy, then code breakdown, then another analogy, then 'why it matters'. No one-liner, and the structure bounces between analogy and detail rather than progressing cleanly.

expectations.text: Includes curated external links with descriptions
expectations.evidence: No external links or resources included.

expectations.text: Technical terms introduced only after being tied to familiar concept
expectations.evidence: 'async' explained as a sign saying 'don't just stand there waiting'. 'await' as 'pause me here'. Terms introduced through analogy.

expectations.text: No single section introduces more than 4 new concepts
expectations.evidence: Code walkthrough goes line by line, one concept each.

expectations.text: References the specific code snippet provided
expectations.evidence: Full line-by-line walkthrough of the original fetchUserData code.
